title: Mark Lamster

infobox.value: This article about an American journalist is a [stub](/wiki/Wikipedia:Stub "Wikipedia:Stub"). You can help Wikipedia by [adding missing information](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Mark_Lamster&action=edit).
(born in New York City[[1]](#cite_note-1)) is an American architecture writer and critic. He writes in the *[Dallas Morning News](/wiki/Dallas_Morning_News "Dallas Morning News")*.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

["Genius and darkness"](https://www.dallasnews.com/arts/architecture/2018/10/31/genius-darkness-mark-lamster-explored-lifeof-architect-philip-johnson-found). *[Dallas Morning News](/wiki/Dallas_Morning_News "Dallas Morning News")*. 31 October 2019.

["Philip Johnson, the Nazi who built a crystal house for himself"](https://www.elpais.com.uy/cultural/philip-johnson-nazi-construyo-casa-cristal.html). *[EL PAIS Cultural](/wiki/El_Pa%C3%ADs_(Uruguay) "El País (Uruguay)")*. 26 May 2019. (in Spanish)

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

American architecture writer and critic

**Mark Lamster** (born in New York City[[1]](#cite_note-1)) is an American architecture writer and critic. He writes in the *[Dallas Morning News](/wiki/Dallas_Morning_News "Dallas Morning News")*.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

In 2018 he wrote a biography, *The Man in the Glass House: Philip Johnson, Architect of the Modern Century*, showing the Nazi past of [Philip Johnson](/wiki/Philip_Johnson "Philip Johnson").[[3]](#cite_note-3)

3. **[^](#cite_ref-3)** ["Philip Johnson, the Nazi who built a crystal house for himself"](https://www.elpais.com.uy/cultural/philip-johnson-nazi-construyo-casa-cristal.html). *[EL PAIS Cultural](/wiki/El_Pa%C3%ADs_(Uruguay) "El País (Uruguay)")*. 26 May 2019. (in Spanish)
